body:
Bead ddx-43d67aa5 is the Story 17 epic. Its sole acceptance criterion is
"All child beads closed." The epic was already decomposed in a prior
planning round into five children:

  - ddx-cd2ecf79 [open]  B17-D: FEAT-018 + FEAT-005 spec amendments
  - ddx-1d52a30c [open]  B17-A: artifacttypes loader + cache + escape guard
  - ddx-9ca4b5bf [open]  B17-B: GraphQL Artifact.typeDefinitions resolver
  - ddx-4fd71cf3 [open]  B17-C1: ArtifactTypePanel.svelte + integration + vitest
  - ddx-9395730c [open]  B17-C2: Playwright e2e + collision fixture + truncation

All five remain open, so the epic's AC cannot be satisfied in this
attempt by definition — the epic closes only after each child closes.

What is done: decomposition (already on disk in the bead store) per the
locked plan at /tmp/story-17-final.md.

What is blocking: the children themselves. The dependency order is
B17-D -> B17-A -> B17-B -> B17-C1 -> B17-C2. The queue should pick up
B17-D (cd2ecf79) next; it has no story-17 deps and lands the spec
contract that B17-A's loader tests assert against.

What a follow-up attempt needs: nothing on this epic directly. After
all five children close, re-queueing this epic will allow it to
auto-close on the verified AC.
